description: No-op: adds an inline comment — both suites must PASS (harness sanity check).
replace: if (!workflowGateId) return null; // null-mutant: no-op

description: Remove bypassed gate from dependency satisfaction; bypassed gates no longer unblock downstream.
replace: gateStates.filter(g => g.status === "passed").map(g => g.gateId), // mutant: bypass-ignored

description: Invert cascadeReset condition — only resets already-pending gates (no-op on passed/failed).
replace: if (gate && gate.status === "pending") { // mutant: inverted condition

description: Dropped signal push — recordSignal() does not append the signal to gate history.
Removed saveNow() from put() — sessions written to memory but never flushed to disk.
Wrong return value — capacity-blocked children falsely report 'started'.
replace:
this._enqueue(rootGoalId, childGoalId);
		return "started"; // mutant: wrong return value

description: Zero debounce — worker idle notifications fire immediately with 0 ms delay (blip protection removed).
replace: private static readonly WORKER_IDLE_NUDGE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 0; // mutant: no debounce

description: Removed statusVersion bump — version stays at 0 forever; clients cannot detect dropped frames.
replace: // session.statusVersion = (session.statusVersion ?? 0) + 1; // mutant: no bump

description: Skip all WS broadcasts — broadcastFrame skips every client unconditionally.
replace: if (true) continue; // mutant: skip all clients

description: Wider slice — toBranchName truncates at 50 chars instead of 14, violating the ≤14 length invariant.
replace: .slice(0, 50) // mutant: wrong truncation

description: Wrong replacement — branchToSlug replaces dots instead of slashes; path components retained.
replace: return branch.replace(/\./g, "-"); // mutant: wrong char replaced

description: Push safety bypass — all git push commands silently approved without checking refspecs.
search:
const tokens = shellTokenize(command);
	const protectedBranches = protectedBranchSet(vars);

description: Timeout wrong — unit command steps get 300 s (same as generic) instead of 1200 s.
replace: return DEFAULT_COMMAND_STEP_TIMEOUT_SEC; // mutant: no unit-command special case

description: DI-fence: gh invocation no longer blocked in execFile — the test-only gh fence is disabled.
note: No legacy catcher — fence is a new v2 seam (demonstrates v2 NEW coverage)

description: DI-fence: NETWORK_GIT_COMMANDS cleared — git push/fetch/clone/ls-remote all allowed through the fence.
replace: const NETWORK_GIT_COMMANDS = new Set<string>([]); // mutant: allow all git network ops
note: No legacy catcher — fence is a new v2 seam (demonstrates v2 NEW coverage)

description: Workflow hidden filter inverted — only hidden workflows returned; visible workflows excluded from cascade.
replace: .filter(w => !!w.hidden) // mutant: inverted hidden filter

description: HQ normalization broken — Headquarters never normalizes to server scope; project-scoped resolution activated for HQ.
replace: return projectId; // mutant: never normalize HQ to server scope

description: Sort reversed — messages sorted descending by _order instead of ascending; newest appears first.
replace: if (a._order !== b._order) return b._order - a._order; // mutant: reversed

description: settle-optimistic inverted — condition flipped; already-at-sentinel rows never re-stamped to chronological position.
replace: if (m._origin === "optimistic" && m._order < OPTIMISTIC_ORDER_BASE) { // mutant: inverted

description: Live sessions filter inverted — archived sessions treated as live; non-archived sessions excluded.
replace: const liveSessions = dedupeSessionsById(sortSessions(input.sessions.filter(s => !!s.archived))); // mutant

description: Renderer: shouldRenderLiveVerification always false — gate-verification-live component never rendered.
search: const shouldRenderLiveVerification = !!(latestSignal && verification && goalId2 && resolvedGateId && signalId);
replace: const shouldRenderLiveVerification = false; // mutant: never render live verification
note: Renderer legacy coverage is a Playwright browser spec (tests/gate-status-renderer.spec.ts) that runs in the browser tier, NOT the node chaos harness — so it is intentionally not listed here to avoid a false-positive kill from a tsx import error. The v2 dom-tier catcher runs the REAL renderer under happy-dom.

description: Archive flag not set — existing.archived stays undefined; callbacks fire on every subsequent archive call (idempotence broken).
Silenced failure — failed gate status returns 'pending' instead of 'failed'; failures invisible to the plan tab.
replace: if (gates.some(g => g.status === "failed")) return "pending"; // mutant: wrong status

description: Bypassed count reads passed gates — bypassedCount reflects passed gates instead of bypassed ones; a workflow with zero bypassed gates reports a non-zero bypassed count.
replace: const bypassed = summaryGates.filter(gate => gate.status === "passed").length; // mutant: counts passed as bypassed

description: Inverted passed predicate — the passed tally counts non-passed gates, so a fully-passed workflow reports 0/N.
replace: passed: summaryGates.filter(gate => gate.status !== "passed").length, // mutant: inverted passed predicate

description: Inverted expiry comparison — pruneExpired retains expired requests and deletes still-valid ones.
replace: const kept = list.filter(m => m.expiresAt < now); // mutant: inverted expiry check

description: Inverted pending filter — listPending returns already-resolved (non-pending) entries and hides genuinely pending ones.
replace: return this.ensureLoaded(staffId).filter((e) => e.state !== "pending"); // mutant: inverted pending filter

description: Off-by-one in acquire — permits are handed out even when the semaphore is saturated (value 0), so the (N+1)th acquire never blocks and concurrency caps are breached.
Off-by-one in release — the over-release guard uses > instead of >=, so releasing at capacity silently exceeds the cap instead of throwing.
replace: if (this._value > this._capacity) { // mutant: off-by-one — over-release no longer throws

description: Inverted step-name guard — every ready-to-merge step EXCEPT 'Master merged into branch' is rewritten to the parent-branch echo, corrupting the push/PR steps for spawned child goals.
replace: if (step.name !== "Master merged into branch") { // mutant: inverted step guard

description: Off-by-one in ring eviction — the buffer evicts one entry early (at length == maxSize), dropping the oldest replayable event before capacity is actually exceeded.
replace: if (this.buffer.length >= this.maxSize) { // mutant: off-by-one eviction

description: Off-by-one in resume window — canResumeFrom drops the +1, so a client whose last-seen seq equals the oldest retained seq minus one is wrongly told it cannot resume (forces a full snapshot / dropped-frame path).
replace: return this.buffer[0].seq <= fromSeq; // mutant: off-by-one resume window

description: Off-by-one in retry backoff exponent — attempt 1 waits 2s instead of 1s (the whole doubling sequence is shifted up one step), doubling provider-overload backoff pressure.
replace: const exponent = Math.min(safeAttempt, 60); // mutant: off-by-one backoff exponent

description: Swapped arguments — parseShortstat returns insertions where deletions belong and vice-versa, so the git status widget shows inverted +/- line counts.
search:
insertions: ins ? parseInt(ins[1], 10) || 0 : 0,
		deletions: del ? parseInt(del[1], 10) || 0 : 0,
replace:
insertions: del ? parseInt(del[1], 10) || 0 : 0, // mutant: swapped ins/del
		deletions: ins ? parseInt(ins[1], 10) || 0 : 0,

description: Negated remote-prefix guard — parseBaseRef treats 'origin/<branch>' as a local branch (no prefix strip, isRemote=false) and local branches as remote, corrupting worktree base-ref resolution.
replace: if (!trimmed.startsWith("origin/")) { // mutant: negated remote-prefix guard

description: Removed await — resolveBaseRefWithExec no longer awaits the symbolic-ref exec, so `out` is a Promise; `.trim()` throws, is swallowed by the surrounding catch, and the resolver silently falls back to the HEAD sentinel instead of the real origin branch.
replace: const out = exec(["symbolic-ref", "refs/remotes/origin/HEAD"]); // mutant: removed await

description: Reversed comparator (swapped operands) — getSortedPhases sorts phases descending, so verification runs phase 2 before phase 0 and the phase-ordering contract breaks.
replace: return [...groups.keys()].sort((a, b) => b - a); // mutant: reversed phase order

description: Off-by-one in cache-completeness check — canSkipAllSteps uses > instead of >=, so a fully-cached step set is never recognized as skippable and re-runs every step.
replace: return cache.size > activeSteps.length && activeSteps.every(s => cache.has(s.name)); // mutant: off-by-one

description: Inverted boolean logic — computeAllPassed requires skipped AND passed instead of skipped OR passed, so any phase-skipped (passed:false) step wrongly fails the whole gate.
replace: return results.every(r => r.skipped && r.passed); // mutant: inverted OR->AND

description: Wrong return value — scopeToOrigin maps the server scope to the 'builtin' origin tag, so server-scoped roles/tools are mislabelled and precedence/override provenance is corrupted.
replace: case "server": return "builtin"; // mutant: wrong origin tag

description: Negated projectId guard — resolveWorkflows returns [] when a projectId IS supplied (and attempts resolution when it is absent), so project/Headquarters workflow resolution returns nothing.
replace: if (projectId) return []; // mutant: negated workflow guard

description: Inverted find predicate — readRoleField matches the first role whose name does NOT equal the requested role, so role model/thinking/prompt resolution returns the wrong role's fields (or undefined).
replace: const r = roles.find(x => x.name !== roleName); // mutant: inverted role lookup

description: Off-by-one in synthetic id — id-less live rows are stamped synth:seq:<seq+1>, so the stable render key no longer matches the broadcast seq and re-delivery/dedup keys drift.
replace: incoming = { ...incoming, id: `synth:seq:${seq + 1}` }; // mutant: off-by-one synth id

description: Inverted replace-by-id lookup — the reducer finds the first row whose id does NOT match the incoming id, so a re-delivered live row is appended as a duplicate instead of replacing in place.
replace: const idx = messages.findIndex((m) => m.id !== incoming.id); // mutant: inverted replace-by-id

description: Dropped alternative (OR->AND) — the optimistic text-fallback requires BOTH the raw text and the skill-expanded modelText to match, so a slash-skill prompt echoed as its expanded body is no longer deduped (S17 double-render regression).
replace: (extractText(m) === text && reconstructModelText(m) === text), // mutant: dropped OR alternative

description: Wrong default value — the default base indentation is changed from the design constant to 3px, so unconfigured sidebars render with the wrong indent metadata.
replace: const DEFAULT_BASE_INDENT_PX = 3; // mutant: wrong default base indent

description: Wrong default value — the default nested-goal indentation is changed to 9px, so nested goal rows render at the wrong depth-scaled indent.
replace: const DEFAULT_NESTED_GOAL_INDENT_PX = 9; // mutant: wrong default nested indent

description: Swapped arguments — the base-indent clamp passes MAX as the min bound and MIN as the max bound, so indentation preferences are clamped to the wrong (inverted) range.
replace: MAX_NESTED_GOAL_INDENT_PX, MIN_NESTED_GOAL_INDENT_PX, DEFAULT_BASE_INDENT_PX

description: Inverted archived filter — the live-goal forest is built from archived goals (and live goals are excluded), so active goals vanish from the sidebar.
replace: const liveGoals = projectGoals.filter(g => !!g.archived); // mutant: inverted archived filter

description: Inverted description guard — when a description IS provided the collapsed header renders the summarized command fallback instead of the italic description (and vice-versa).
replace: ? (!params.description // mutant: inverted description guard
note: Renderer legacy coverage is a Playwright browser spec (tests/bash-renderer-description.spec.ts) that runs in the browser tier, not the node chaos harness; the v2 dom-tier catcher renders the REAL BashRenderer under happy-dom.

description: Inverted truncation detection — isTruncated returns the opposite verdict, so a truncated write result is rendered as normal content (no preview / no 'Load full content' affordance).
replace: (content as any)._truncated !== true // mutant: inverted truncation detection
note: Renderer legacy coverage is a Playwright browser spec (tests/write-renderer-truncated.spec.ts) that runs in the browser tier, not the node chaos harness; the v2 dom-tier catcher renders the REAL WriteRenderer under happy-dom.

description: Wrong divisor — formatSize divides megabyte-scale sizes by 1024 instead of 1048576, so the truncation badge reports a wildly wrong size (e.g. '10240.0 MB' instead of '10.0 MB').
replace: if (bytes >= 1_048_576) return `${(bytes / 1024).toFixed(1)} MB`; // mutant: wrong MB divisor
note: Renderer legacy coverage is a Playwright browser spec (tests/write-renderer-truncated.spec.ts) that runs in the browser tier, not the node chaos harness; the v2 dom-tier catcher renders the REAL WriteRenderer under happy-dom.

description: Dropped terminal-status branch — the live gate verification component only forwards a 'failed' finalStatus (the 'passed' case collapses to undefined), so a passed gate never shows its terminal state.
replace: signalStatus === "failed" ? signalStatus : undefined
note: Renderer legacy coverage is a Playwright browser spec (tests/gate-status-renderer.spec.ts) that runs in the browser tier, not the node chaos harness; the v2 dom-tier catcher renders the REAL GateStatusRenderer under happy-dom.

description: DI-fence: inverted loopback guard — the fenced fetch would BLOCK loopback hosts and ALLOW non-loopback ones, disabling the fail-closed outbound-HTTP fence (a test could reach the network).
replace: if (isLoopbackHost(url.hostname)) { // mutant: inverted loopback fence
note: No legacy catcher — the fetch fence is a new v2 DI seam (demonstrates v2 NEW coverage). Pins fence-per-seam: at least one mutant disables each fence (command-runner: M13/M14; fetch: M53).
